title: 🧩subworkflow - aws restart ec2 instance
description:
Subworkflow to restart an EC2 instance using AWS Systems Manager Automation

This subworkflow provides an easy way to restart an EC2 instance via the AWS-RestartEC2Instance SSM automation document.
Waits until the restart has finished and checks if it was successful.

Parameters:

instanceId: (Required) The EC2 instance ID to restart
awsregion: (Required) The AWS region where the instance resides
additionalInstanceIds: (Optional) Additional instance IDs to restart
forceStop: (Optional) Force stop the instance before restarting (true/false)
dynatraceawsconnection: (Required) Dynatrace AWS OIDC connection name
guide:
## 🔄 **Subworkflow to Restart an EC2 Instance**

♻️ This subworkflow provides an **easy and abstracted way** to **restart a running EC2 instance**—without requiring any knowledge of how AWS Systems Manager (SSM) works.

It executes the restart via the **AWS-RestartEC2Instance** SSM automation document, **waits for the restart to complete**, and **validates whether the restart was successful**.  
❌ If the restart fails, the subworkflow fails as well, allowing the **parent workflow to restart on error**.

---

### ✅ **Requirements**

⚠️ For this subworkflow to run successfully:
- The EC2 instance **must be in the running state**
- The EC2 instance **must be managed by AWS Systems Manager (SSM)**

📖 AWS documentation on configuring SSM-managed instances:  
https://docs.aws.amazon.com/systems-manager/latest/userguide/systems-manager-setting-up-ec2.html

---

### ⚙️ **Parameters**

- **instanceId** (Required): 🆔 The EC2 instance ID to restart.  
- **awsregion** (Required): 🌍 The AWS region where the EC2 instance resides.  
- **additionalInstanceIds** (Optional): 🆔 Additional instance IDs to restart (comma-separated).  
- **forceStop** (Optional): 🛑 Force stop the instance before restarting (true/false). Default: false.  
- **dynatraceawsconnection** (Required): 🔑 The Dynatrace AWS OIDC connection used to authenticate with AWS.

---

### 📘 **AWS SSM Document Used**

This subworkflow uses the AWS-managed automation document:

**Document Name**: `AWS-RestartEC2Instance`

🔗 AWS Documentation:  
https://docs.aws.amazon.com/systems-manager-automation-runbooks/latest/userguide/automation-aws-restartec2instance.html

**What this runbook does:**
- Restarts one or more EC2 instances
- Optionally forces a stop before restarting
- Waits for instance to reach running state
- Verifies successful restart

---

### 🧠 **Workflow Logic**

- 📤 The subworkflow sends a restart request to the AWS SSM automation document **`AWS-RestartEC2Instance`**
- ♻️ The specified EC2 instance(s) are restarted
- ⏳ The subworkflow waits for the restart operation to complete  
- ✅ If successful, the subworkflow completes and returns the restart status
- ❌ If the restart fails, the subworkflow fails to enable **restart-on-error** handling in the parent workflow

---

start-automation.name: start-automation
start-automation.description: Initiate EC2 instance restart via SSM
